req.title: Diagnostic report (boundary)
req.statement: A clinician shall issue a DiagnosticReport referencing Observations; missing Observation links shall be rejected. Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-037 mandates that a DiagnosticReport must reference at least one Observation, and that boundary conditions on timestamps and numeric elements are exercised. I derived four tests: (1) EP positive – a well-formed report with valid Observation references returns 201; (2) NEG negative – a report with an empty 'result' array is rejected with 422; (3) BVA boundary – a timestamp at the minimum representable FHIR dateTime (1900-01-01T00:00:00Z) and a numeric valueQuantity at the IEEE 754 double boundary are accepted; (4) DT decision-table – a report where the 'result' field is absent entirely (vs. empty array) also yields 422, confirming both rejection branches of the reference-presence rule.

tests.name: test_diagnostic_report_valid_observation_reference
preconditions: A valid Observation resource exists; its ID is returned by <helper_fn:create_observation>
actions: POST /DiagnosticReport with resourceType, status, code, subject, effectiveDateTime, and result array containing one Observation reference
expected: Response body result array is non-empty
trace: R-HLT-037: clinician issues report -> report stored
tests.executable:
import pytest, requests
from conftest import BASE_URL

def test_diagnostic_report_valid_observation_reference(clinician_auth_headers, create_observation):
    obs_id = create_observation()
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "DiagnosticReport",
        "status": "final",
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "58410-2"}]},
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/test-patient-1"},
        "effectiveDateTime": "2024-06-15T10:00:00Z",
        "result": [{"reference": f"Observation/{obs_id}"}]
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/DiagnosticReport",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "DiagnosticReport"
    assert len(body.get("result", [])) >= 1

tests.name: test_diagnostic_report_empty_result_rejected
actions: POST /DiagnosticReport with an empty 'result' array (no Observation references)
expected: Response body contains an OperationOutcome or error message indicating missing Observation links
trace: R-HLT-037: missing Observation links shall be rejected -> 422 when references empty
tests.executable:
import requests
from conftest import BASE_URL

def test_diagnostic_report_empty_result_rejected(clinician_auth_headers):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "DiagnosticReport",
        "status": "final",
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "58410-2"}]},
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/test-patient-1"},
        "effectiveDateTime": "2024-06-15T10:00:00Z",
        "result": []
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/DiagnosticReport",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    text = str(body)
    assert any(kw in text.lower() for kw in ["result", "observation", "reference", "invalid"])

tests.name: test_diagnostic_report_boundary_timestamp_and_numeric
preconditions: An Observation with a numeric valueQuantity near the IEEE 754 max double exists via <helper_fn:create_observation_with_value>
actions: POST /DiagnosticReport with effectiveDateTime set to minimum FHIR-allowed dateTime '1900-01-01T00:00:00Z'
Include result reference to an Observation whose valueQuantity.value is 1.7976931348623157e+308 (IEEE 754 max double)
expected: Linked Observation numeric value stored without overflow or truncation
trace: R-HLT-037: Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested
tests.executable:
import requests
from conftest import BASE_URL

MIN_DATETIME = "1900-01-01T00:00:00Z"
MAX_DOUBLE = 1.7976931348623157e+308

def test_diagnostic_report_boundary_timestamp_and_numeric(
    clinician_auth_headers, create_observation_with_value
):
    obs_id = create_observation_with_value(value=MAX_DOUBLE, unit="mg/dL")
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "DiagnosticReport",
        "status": "final",
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "58410-2"}]},
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/test-patient-1"},
        "effectiveDateTime": MIN_DATETIME,
        "result": [{"reference": f"Observation/{obs_id}"}]
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/DiagnosticReport",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("effectiveDateTime") == MIN_DATETIME
    assert len(body.get("result", [])) == 1

tests.name: test_diagnostic_report_absent_result_field_rejected
actions: POST /DiagnosticReport omitting the 'result' field entirely (field not present, distinct from empty array)
expected: Response body signals that Observation references are required
trace: R-HLT-037: missing Observation links shall be rejected -> 422 when references empty
Decision table row: result=absent -> reject (complements result=[] -> reject row)
tests.executable:
import requests
from conftest import BASE_URL

def test_diagnostic_report_absent_result_field_rejected(clinician_auth_headers):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "DiagnosticReport",
        "status": "final",
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "58410-2"}]},
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/test-patient-1"},
        "effectiveDateTime": "2024-06-15T10:00:00Z"
    }
    assert "result" not in payload, "result must be absent to test this branch"
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/DiagnosticReport",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    text = str(body)
    assert any(kw in text.lower() for kw in ["result", "observation", "reference", "required"])
